explanation: The natural language statement specifies an exhaustive list of operational modes for the controller. This LTL property captures that the controller must always be in one of these defined modes. It asserts that at any point in time (globally), the system is either in Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, Standby Mode (VentilationOff), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, or Fail-safe Mode. This ensures that the controller operates strictly within the specified set of functionalities.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies the set of operational modes that the controller 'shall implement'. This implies that the controller must always be in one of these defined modes. The LTL property `G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)` captures this by stating that 'globally' (G), at all times, the system must be in the Start-up Mode, OR Self-Test Mode, OR Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff), OR Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, OR Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, OR fail-safe mode. The disjunction (`|`) covers all the specified modes, ensuring that at any given moment, the system's state falls within one of these categories.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The statement "The controller shall implement the following modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff, Pressure Controlled Venilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, fail-safe" implies that the controller is always in one of these specified operational modes. The LTL formula captures this by using the 'G' (Globally) operator to assert that at all times, the system must be in at least one of the listed modes. The modes 'VentilationOff' is mapped to 'StandbyMode' as per its description. The disjunction ('|') ensures that at any point, one of these modes is active.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement describes the complete set of operational modes the controller is designed to implement. This means that at any point in time, the controller must be in one of these specified modes. The LTL property captures this by using the global operator (G) to assert that always, the controller is in the Start-up Mode, or the Self-Test Mode, or the Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff), or the Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, or the Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, or the Fail-safe Mode. The disjunction (OR) between these atomic propositions ensures that at least one of these modes is active at any given moment, thus defining the full set of permissible operational states for the controller.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies a set of distinct operational modes that the controller 'shall implement'. This implies that at any point in time, the controller must be operating in one of these listed modes. The LTL property captures this by using the 'G' (Globally) operator combined with a disjunction ('|') of all specified modes. This ensures that the system is always in one of the defined modes (Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff/Standby Mode, Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, or Fail-safe Mode).
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies a set of modes that the controller 'shall implement'. This implies that the controller must always be in one of these defined modes. Therefore, the LTL property asserts that globally (G), the system is always in either Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (represented by StandbyMode), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, or fail-safe mode.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies an exhaustive list of modes the controller shall implement. This implies that at any given point in time, the controller must be in one of these specified modes. The LTL formula captures this by using the 'Globally' (G) operator to assert that throughout the system's execution, a disjunction (OR) of all the listed modes must always be true. Each atomic proposition represents one of the specified modes: 'StartUpMode' for 'Start-up Mode', 'SelfTestMode' for 'Self-Test Mode', 'StandbyMode' (which is described as 'same as VentilationOff Mode') for 'VentilationOff', 'PCVMode' for 'Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode', 'PSVMode' for 'Pressure Support Ventilation Mode', and 'failSafeMode' for 'fail-safe'.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies a list of modes that the controller 'shall implement'. This implies that the controller must always be in one of these specified modes at any given time. The LTL translation captures this by using the 'G' (Globally) operator combined with a disjunction ('|') of all the identified mode propositions. This ensures that throughout the system's operation, it is always active in one of the Start-up, Self-Test, Standby (VentilationOff), Pressure Controlled Ventilation, Pressure Support Ventilation, or Fail-safe modes.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The natural language statement specifies an exhaustive list of modes the controller is expected to implement. This means that at any given point in time, the controller must be operating in one of these specified modes. The 'G' (globally) operator is used to assert that this condition must hold true at all times. The disjunction (logical OR, represented by '|') of all identified modes ensures that the system is always in at least one of them. The modes identified from the atomic propositions are StartUpMode, SelfTestMode, StandbyMode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff), PCVMode, PSVMode, and failSafeMode.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)

explanation: The LTL property states that at any given point in time (Globally, G), the controller must be in one of the specified operational modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, or Fail-Safe Mode. This captures the natural language statement 'The controller shall implement the following modes' by asserting that the controller's operational state is always confined to this defined set of modes. It implies that no other modes are active or possible at any time. This property does not explicitly enforce mutual exclusivity between the modes, only that one of them must always be true.
output_LTL: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)
